question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 10.00% on 2025-12-21 for the Metaculus question "Will at least one All-Star from the NFL, NBA, MLB, or NHL be banned or suspended for sports betting before 2026?"?
summary: The CP on the underlying question is exactly at the 10.00% threshold, and the meta-question requires it to be strictly 'higher than' 10.00%. No All-Star player has been suspended for betting in 2025 — the NBA gambling scandal involves Rozier (not an All-Star) and Billups (a coach, excluded by fine print). With ~3 weeks remaining and no qualifying event, the rational direction for CP is flat-to-down. However, the extreme boundary sensitivity means even minor fluctuations could resolve this Yes. I estimate 35% probability the CP drifts above 10.00% by December 21.

factors.description: CP is exactly at the 10.00% threshold; status quo (no change) resolves No since question requires 'higher than'
No All-Star player suspended for betting in 2025 - Rozier isn't an All-Star, Billups is a coach (excluded by fine print)
Only ~3 weeks remain for underlying question - rational forecasters should update downward as deadline approaches with no event
Boundary sensitivity: CP is right at 10.00%, so even tiny upward fluctuations from new/updated forecasts could push it above threshold
Active NBA gambling scandal keeps topic visible; some forecasters may incorrectly think Billups (an All-Star coach) qualifies under resolution criteria
Weighted median by recency means recent forecasts have outsized impact - a few new higher forecasts could nudge CP above 10%
